meaning: to be boring; to be bored

expressions.english: boring book
boring movie
boring class
boring person
You look bored.

Were you not bored?
Was it okay (and not boring)?
Is my story boring you?
That class is too boring.
Today has been very boring.
I have nothing to do today, so I'm bored.
We had a boring meeting for three long hours.
expressions.notes:
회의 = meeting
셋 = three (native Korean number)
-이나 = as many as + number; as much as + amount
세 is the adjective form of 셋.

expressions.english: I have never watched a drama that's THIS boring.
expressions.notes:
이렇게 = like this, in this manner; so + adjective
드라마 = TV drama, soap opera
처음 = first; for the first time

expressions.korean: 요즘은 너무 바빠서 지루할 틈이 없어요.
expressions.english: I've been so busy recently that there is no time to be bored.
expressions.notes: -(으)은 틈이 없다 = there is no time to + V

expressions.english: It's boring in the beginning, but it gets more interesting later.
expressions.notes:
나중 = later
재미있다 = to be fun; to be funny; to be interesting

expressions.english: The movie was so boring that I fell asleep while I was watching it.

expressions.korean: 지루하면 잠깐 밖에 나가서 바람 좀 쐬고 와요.
expressions.english: If you are bored, go out and get some fresh air.
expressions.notes:
잠깐 = short time; for a moment
밖 = outside
나가다 = to go out
바람 = wind
바람을 쐬다 = to get some fresh air

expressions.korean: 그 일은 돈 많이 주는데 지루하고 재미가 없어요.
expressions.english: That job pays you well, but it's boring and not fun.
expressions.notes:
돈 = money
주다 = to give
재미가 없다 = to be not fun; to be not funny; to be not interesting; to be boring

expressions.korean: 아무리 지루해도 수업 시간에는 잠을 자면 안 돼요.
expressions.english: No matter how boring it is, you shouldn't sleep during class.
expressions.notes:
아무리 -어/아/여도 = no matter how + adjective + S + V
자다 = to sleep
잠을 자다 = to sleep

expressions.korean: 이 영화 지루할 것 같았는데 생각보다 재미있어요.
expressions.english: I thought this movie would be boring, but it was more interesting than I thought.

expressions.korean: 이 책, 재미있을 것 같아서 샀는데 읽어보니까 지루해요.
expressions.english: I bought this book because it looked interesting, but I've read some of it, and I find it boring.
expressions.notes:
읽다 = to read
-어/아/여 보니까 = once + S + V-ed; it turns out that ...
